notes.anchor: We were two hours at Thyrreum
notes.note: Thyrreum, an inland town of Acarnania in western Greece, was a brief halt on Cicero's homeward route; the dating shows he was making rapid progress along the coast.
Xenomenes is a Greek friend of Tiro's at Thyrreum who offered hospitality and help; "host" (hospes) marks a formal tie of guest-friendship.

notes.anchor: Acastus is down at the harbour every day
notes.note: Acastus is one of Cicero's slave-couriers, posted at the port so that letters from Tiro could be picked up and forwarded by passing travellers.

notes.anchor: My whole hope for your being carefully looked after rests in Curius
notes.note: Manius Curius, the Roman banker at Patrae, was the man Cicero most trusted to oversee Tiro's care; Cicero urges Tiro to put himself entirely in his hands.
